h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to read it like a mini sentence
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can submit that, but I still need to look at the place before I trust the label.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward simple confirmation that the three items are correctly sorted, with the learner gradually accepting the position rule through repetition.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New submit gate: cover the words. Fill the slots first: Slot 1: top of column = ______ Slot 2: inside row = ______ Slot 3: inside row = ______
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L2 signals persistent risk of reading the word before checking position ('I might still mark by the word instead of the position'), T3 abandons the verbal-sorting repetition and invents a slot-filling mechanism that physically occludes the words, forcing positional reasoning before label attachment.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The word sort is settled. The remaining check is earlier: slot before word.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 acknowledges L3's reframing ('the word sort is settled') and redirects the task entirely to the residual vulnerability L3 named ('I still need to look at the place before I trust the label'), shifting from sorting to slot-first verification.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to read it like a mini sentence

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The word sort is settled. The remaining check is earlier: slot before word.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T3 acknowledges L3's reframing ('the word sort is settled') and redirects the task entirely to the residual vulnerability L3 named ('I still need to look at the place before I trust the label'), shifting from sorting to slot-first verification.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: After L2 signals persistent risk of reading the word before checking position ('I might still mark by the word instead of the position'), T3 abandons the verbal-sorting repetition and invents a slot-filling mechanism that physically occludes the words, forcing positional reasoning before label attachment.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New submit gate: cover the words. Fill the slots first: Slot 1: top of column = ______ Slot 2: inside row = ______ Slot 3: inside row = ______
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: After L2 signals persistent risk of reading the word before checking position ('I might still mark by the word instead of the position'), T3 abandons the verbal-sorting repetition and invents a slot-filling mechanism that physically occludes the words, forcing positional reasoning before label attachment.

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the card names the thing acting on the cart first, not the motion.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The table card is not a second force here; it is another name trying to use the same contact.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to resolve the surface/table ambiguity by choosing one card and confirming the labeling principle already stated in L1 and L2.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card choice has stopped settling this. The blank is now the test.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L2 keeps cycling between surface and table without resolution, T3 abandons the card-placement task entirely and introduces the fill-in-the-blank strip as a new representational object that forces a single-answer commitment, constituting a genuine shift in task and evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: So the card names the thing acting on the cart first, not the motion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: After L2 keeps cycling between surface and table without resolution, T3 abandons the card-placement task entirely and introduces the fill-in-the-blank strip as a new representational object that forces a single-answer commitment, constituting a genuine shift in task and evidence standard.
